title: Create Control Change
content:
# Create Control Change

Send a new value to be written to an Asset / Data Stream pair. The Control Change Manager will automatically find which Cluster and Bridge to communicate the change to.

## Endpoint

`POST /control-changes/create`

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key")
response = client.control_change.createControlChange()
print(response)

expiration_date.description: UTC time when the Control Change will expire and the `status` automatically marked as `failed`, formatted in RFC 3339.

from.description: Information about the current `resource` data when the the Control Change `payload` is created.

value.description: Value represented in one of these formats.

timestamp.description: UTC time when the current `value` is recorded in the Control Change object, formatted in RFC 3339.

payload.description: Value represented in one of these formats.

resource.description: The asset / data stream pair that this Control Change will be applied to.

retries.description: How many times the Control Change Manager will try and send the same Control Change request to the Bridge before the change is tagged `failed` and no further attempts will be made. If the Bridge sends a `processed` acknowledgment, then the Control Change Manager will stop any further retries and wait for an `applied` response.

timeout.description: How long the Control Change Manager will wait in seconds for the Bridge to send a `processed` acknowledgement before a retry will be attempted. If the total number of retries has reach its `retries` limit, then the change is tagged `failed` and no further attempts will be made.

created_by.description: Name of the process that created the Control Change. This could be a user, workload, application, etc.

created_type.description: Type of process that created the Control Change. This is inferred from `source`.

id.description: A unique randomly generated UUID as the key `id` for the Control Change.

payload.description: Value represented in one of these formats.

resource.description: The asset / data stream pair that this Control Change will be applied to.

source.description: The process that created the Control Change request. This can be a user or an automated process like a workload, application, recommendation, etc.

timestamp.description: UTC time when the log was created, formatted in RFC 3339.

trace_id.description: This is for internal purposes and is the same as the `id`.
